rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical synthesis of vinyl sulfones from sulfonyl hydrazides and styrenes using KI as redox mediator in an undivided cell
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of iodide to I2 (and to p-toluenesulfonyl iodide A); the sulfonyl iodide A is reduced at the cathode to sulfinate anion B which is then re-oxidized at the anode to sulfonyl radical C; benzyl radical D is further oxidized at the anode to benzyl cation E. H2O is reduced at the cathode to H2 and OH-.
electron_flow: At the anode, iodide is oxidized to I2 which converts TsNHNH2 into sulfonyl iodide A; A is reduced at the cathode via ECE to sulfinate B which is then anodically oxidized to sulfonyl radical C. C adds to styrene to give benzyl radical D, which is anodically oxidized to cation E; loss of H+ (or HI via iodosulfone F) yields the vinyl sulfone.

probe_reagents.name: p-toluenesulfonyl iodide A (isolated/prepared)
probe_reagents.observation: Independently prepared; its CV shows irreversible cathodic reduction (~-0.75 V vs SCE) consistent with ECE reduction to sulfinate

probe_reagents.name: sodium p-toluenesulfinate (TsNa)
probe_reagents.observation: CV shows irreversible oxidation at +0.78 V; current matches 1e- (vs ferrocene) confirming single-electron oxidation to sulfonyl radical C

probe_reagents.name: ferrocene
probe_reagents.observation: Used as 1e- reference to calibrate the electron count of the sulfinate oxidation

probe_reagents.name: β-hydroxysulfone 4ao
probe_reagents.observation: Electrolysis under reaction conditions did NOT give vinyl sulfone 3ao, ruling out dehydroxylation pathway

probe_reagents.name: p-methoxystyrene / α-methylstyrene
probe_reagents.observation: Yielded β-hydroxysulfones 4, supporting intermediacy of stabilized benzyl cation E

notes: Two pathways from cation E are proposed: (i) direct deprotonation to vinyl sulfone, or (ii) iodide trapping to β-iodosulfone F followed by HI elimination. With electron-rich styrenes, E is trapped by water to give β-hydroxysulfones (off-pathway, control experiment shows these do not dehydrate to vinyl sulfones). Hypoiodite/iodate species formed from I2 + cathodically generated OH- may also participate in oxidizing the hydrazide.
